articles.headline: DK Shivakumar unanimously elected as the next Chief Minister of Karnataka
articles.summary: DK Shivakumar was elected leader of the Congress Legislature Party in Karnataka, succeeding Siddaramaiah. The decision followed internal power-sharing talks overseen by the Congress central leadership. Shivakumar has staked his claim to form the government, with the oath of office scheduled for June 3 at 4:05 pm at the Lok Bhavan in Bengaluru.

articles.headline: NTA announces CUET retest for 3,700 students following technical glitches
articles.summary: The National Testing Agency will conduct a retest for over 3,700 candidates after a system error at a Noida hub delayed the Common University Entrance Test (CUET). Officials attributed the disruption to system failures at TCS-iON. Separately, CBSE’s revaluation portal faced a cyberattack affecting payment processing for students, though no data breach occurred.

articles.headline: Siddaramaiah’s son Yathindra seeks cabinet post in new Karnataka government
articles.summary: Outgoing Karnataka CM Siddaramaiah’s son, Yathindra, has expressed his aspiration for a ministerial post. He claimed that senior Congress leaders, including Rahul Gandhi, suggested his induction. His request is reportedly part of a 2023 power-sharing agreement between Siddaramaiah and incoming CM DK Shivakumar to rotate leadership midway through the term.

articles.headline: Four-storey building collapses in Delhi’s Saidulajab; search for trapped persons ongoing
articles.summary: A four-storey building under construction in Saidulajab, south Delhi, collapsed onto an adjacent canteen on Saturday evening. At least eight people were rescued and hospitalized at AIIMS, while four to six others, including a canteen worker, are feared trapped. Delhi Fire Services and police are conducting rescue operations at the site near Saket Metro station.

articles.headline: US warns of readiness for Iran war as peace deal stalls
articles.summary: The US has warned it is capable of resuming war with Iran after President Donald Trump insisted any peace deal must adhere to red lines, including a total ban on nuclear weapons development. While the White House signaled a potential agreement, Tehran denied a final deal was reached. US officials stated the decision is currently waiting on Trump's final approval.

articles.headline: India signs BrahMos missile deal with Vietnam; Indonesia pact in progress
articles.summary: Defence Secretary Rajesh Kumar Singh confirmed India has signed a BrahMos missile deal with Vietnam, though not yet publicly announced. A similar agreement with Indonesia is in the final stages. The move marks an expansion of India’s defense exports to ASEAN nations facing maritime disputes with China in the South China Sea.

articles.headline: Outgoing Navy Chief says West Asia conflict debunks myth of short wars
articles.summary: Admiral Dinesh K Tripathi stated the West Asia conflict proves that maritime and economic security are inseparable and debunks the idea of swift wars. He emphasized the rise of uncrewed systems and AI-driven naval strategy. Tripathi also noted that military theaterisation is essential to align India’s strategic vision and combat capabilities.

articles.headline: General NS Raja Subramani to take charge as next CDS today
articles.summary: General NS Raja Subramani will succeed General Anil Chauhan as India’s Chief of Defence Staff on Sunday. He is expected to fast-track the establishment of joint services commands, a key part of India's military theaterisation drive. The reform aims to integrate assets from the army, navy, and air force under unified theater commanders.

articles.headline: TMC’s Abhishek Banerjee attacked with stones and eggs in Sonarpur
articles.summary: Trinamool Congress general secretary Abhishek Banerjee sustained minor injuries after being pelted with stones and eggs during a visit to victims of post-poll violence. He was forced to wear a helmet to escape the mob. TMC leaders blamed the BJP for the incident, while the opposition called it a sign of public anger and a breakdown of law and order.

articles.headline: Court orders money laundering charges against Sukesh Chandrashekar and Jacqueline Fernandez
articles.summary: A Delhi court ordered the framing of charges against conman Sukesh Chandrashekar and actor Jacqueline Fernandez in a ₹200 crore extortion case. The judge found sufficient material to proceed under the PMLA and MCOCA. The court noted Fernandez was allegedly aware of the gifts arising from the proceeds of crime, a matter to be decided during the trial.

articles.headline: Maharashtra FDA seizes Patanjali products in crackdown on misleading ads
articles.summary: The Maharashtra FDA seized misbranded drug stocks worth ₹73.24 lakh, including products worth ₹51.41 lakh from Patanjali’s Divya Pharmacy. The state-wide crackdown targeted products promoted with misleading advertisements claiming cures for serious ailments. Seized items include eye drops and herbal tablets across various regional divisions.

articles.headline: Supreme Court rules child’s right to know parentage outweighs privacy concerns
articles.summary: The Supreme Court dismissed a man’s appeal against a DNA test, ruling that a child’s quest for identity can trump a father’s right to privacy. The bench stated that in paternity disputes where no other definitive evidence is available, courts must balance competing rights to ensure a child is not denied legal and identity-based entitlements.

articles.headline: Satwik-Chirag stun top seeds to reach Singapore Open final
articles.summary: India's Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty defeated world No. 1 pair Kim Won Ho and Seo Seung Jae of Korea in straight games to enter the Singapore Open men’s doubles final. The victory ended the Koreans' 34-match winning streak. They will face Indonesians Fajar Alfian and Muhammad Shohibul Fikri in the summit clash.

articles.headline: PSG beat Arsenal in shootout to retain Champions League crown
articles.summary: Paris Saint-Germain won back-to-back Champions League titles by defeating Arsenal 4-3 on penalties in Budapest. The final ended 1-1 after regulation and extra time, with Ousmane Dembélé equalizing Kai Havertz's early goal. Arsenal’s Gabriel Magalhaes missed the decisive final penalty, handing the victory to Luis Enrique’s side.

articles.headline: Congress assesses political risk in Karnataka following Siddaramaiah’s exit
articles.summary: The transition of power in Karnataka from Siddaramaiah to DK Shivakumar is being watched for its impact on the AHINDA social coalition. Analysts note that while the transition was more dignified than historical leadership changes in the state, the Congress risks unsettling the backward classes and minorities who see Siddaramaiah as their principal leader.

articles.headline: BJP demands audit of ₹5,000 crore in Gruha Lakshmi scheme funds
articles.summary: BJP MLA S Suresh Kumar urged outgoing CM Siddaramaiah to clarify the status of ₹5,000 crore meant for the Gruha Lakshmi welfare scheme. The BJP alleges that payments for February and March 2025 have not reached beneficiaries. The MLA called for an honest explanation regarding the public money before the chief minister demits office.

articles.headline: Former BRS MP Balka Suman arrested for inciting violence
articles.summary: Hyderabad police arrested former BRS MP Balka Suman for allegedly calling on workers to destroy public property and burn offices at Singareni Collieries. Suman’s remarks were made during a party meeting on May 26. He faces charges of criminal intimidation and mischief. Suman claimed the arrest was a political move to silence dissent by the Congress government.

articles.headline: Manhunt for former YSRCP MLA Bolla Brahma Naidu in land grab case
articles.summary: Telangana police are searching for former MLA Bolla Brahma Naidu and a relative for allegedly attempting to grab 10 acres of government land worth ₹1,000 crore. The accused used forged documents to claim ownership and tried to sell the land at discounted rates. Three other individuals have been arrested in connection with the fraudulent land deal at Gandipet.

articles.headline: AIADMK seeks CBI probe into alleged horse-trading by TVK
articles.summary: The AIADMK has requested the Tamil Nadu Governor to order a CBI probe into alleged "horse-trading" by the ruling TVK. The complaint follows the resignation of four AIADMK MLAs who subsequently joined the TVK. AIADMK leaders allege that large sums of money were used to lure the legislators, reflecting a decay in political ethics.

articles.headline: DMK slams TVK minister for allegedly insulting priests during inspection
articles.summary: DMK MLA Anitha Radhakrishnan criticized Minister S Ramesh for allegedly insulting the Trishasundara Archagar priest community at Tiruchendur temple. The minister conducted a surprise inspection in disguise and suspended two staff members. The DMK claimed the traditional priests were humiliated under the guise of investigating mismanagement and illegal fees.

articles.headline: Tamil Nadu farmers protest planned bhoomi puja for Mekedatu dam
articles.summary: Farmers in Tamil Nadu staged protests in Thanjavur against Karnataka’s plan to hold a groundbreaking ceremony for the Mekedatu dam. Led by PR Pandian, the groups argued the project is illegal and violates Supreme Court orders on Cauvery water rights. They warned the dam would snatch drinking water from 32 districts and threatened a rally to the site.

articles.headline: Fatal chemical mix-up suspected in Pune hooch tragedy; 16 dead
articles.summary: Pune investigators believe a fatal mix-up between toxic methanol and ethanol caused a hooch tragedy that killed 16 people. The prime accused reportedly lacked the technical knowledge to distinguish the chemicals. The Maharashtra government has suspended 22 officials, and the FDA has seized nearly 6,000 kg of toxic methanol from a supplier in Bhiwandi.

articles.headline: Bihar government suspends two senior IAS officers on corruption charges
articles.summary: The Bihar government suspended IAS officers Yogesh Kumar Sagar and Abhilasha Kumari Sharma following recommendations from the Enforcement Directorate and Special Vigilance Unit. The officers are facing corruption investigations. The General Administration Department issued the orders under All India Services disciplinary rules for the 2017 and 2014 batch officers.

articles.headline: Amit Shah directs DMs to report demographic changes in border districts
articles.summary: Union Home Minister Amit Shah has ordered district magistrates in border regions to monitor and report demographic changes caused by illegal immigration. During a security review in Gujarat, Shah also directed the formation of Security Coordination Groups to track threats from drones and narcotics and ensure the identification of infiltrators.

articles.headline: RTI activist Simaranjit Singh shot dead in Punjab’s Phagwara
articles.summary: Jalandhar-based RTI activist Simaranjit Singh was shot dead by unidentified gunmen in Kapurthala district. Police recovered a pistol near the body and a rifle in a nearby SUV. Singh had recently filed a plea against the constitutional validity of an anti-sacrilege law. Opposition leaders condemned the killing as a failure of the state’s law and order.

articles.headline: Finance Ministry flags inflation risks from fuel hikes and energy volatility
articles.summary: The Union Finance Ministry’s monthly review warned of risks to the inflation outlook due to recent fuel price increases and global energy price volatility. While retail inflation is currently contained within the RBI's target, a sharp rise in wholesale prices to 8.3% and a depreciating rupee have triggered calls for sustained policy vigilance.

articles.headline: Government exempts customs duty on cotton imports until October
articles.summary: The government has waived customs duty on cotton imports from June 1 to October 31, 2026, to ensure raw material availability at competitive rates for the textile industry. The move, aimed at supporting small and medium enterprises, is expected to make Indian apparel exports more competitive globally amidst rising input costs and international headwinds.

articles.headline: Supreme Court warns against excessive judicial intervention in arbitration
articles.summary: The Supreme Court noted that while arbitration in India is viable, courts sometimes fail the process by allowing excessive intervention. The bench emphasized the importance of finality in arbitral awards and stated that judicial interference often creates problems rather than solving them, impacting India’s ease of doing business and commercial certainty.

articles.headline: CBSE revaluation portal hit by malicious cyberattack; process deferred
articles.summary: A cyberattack on the CBSE revaluation portal disrupted payment processing for students, displaying incorrect fees. The board deferred the next phase of re-evaluation until the system is secured on June 1. Officials clarified that no student data was breached, and experts from IIT Madras and IIT Kanpur are auditing the system to prevent further disruptions.

articles.headline: CCPA fines Vajiram and Ravi IAS centre for misleading advertisements
articles.summary: The Central Consumer Protection Authority imposed a ₹7 lakh penalty on Vajiram and Ravi IAS Study Centre for misleading advertisements. The institute allegedly suggested successful UPSC candidates were classroom students when most had only enrolled in free interview guidance. The CCPA ruled that concealing such details deprives consumers of informed choices.

articles.headline: Mumbai man arrested for allegedly killing son over milk cries
articles.summary: A 27-year-old man in Mumbai's Powai was arrested for the murder of his four-year-old son. In a fit of rage after the child cried for milk, the unemployed accused reportedly slammed the boy against a door and floor, causing fatal injuries. The child, a special needs student, was declared dead on arrival at Rajawadi Hospital.

articles.headline: Chief Election Commissioner defends accuracy of revised electoral rolls
articles.summary: CEC Gyanesh Kumar stated that the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) has produced the most accurate electoral rolls globally. Dismissing opposition criticism, he noted that 1.1 million officials were involved in the process, which saw 9.1 million names removed in Bengal alone. The Supreme Court recently upheld the SIR as a legitimate measure for fair elections.

articles.headline: Four detained after shooting at Himachal Pradesh hotel
articles.summary: Kullu police detained four individuals from Punjab following a shooting at Hotel Green Valley in Kasol. The incident occurred during a brawl between tourists and local youth. While shots were allegedly fired during the physical altercation, no fatalities were reported. One suspect remains at large, while search operations continue.

articles.headline: Jaishankar meets Myanmar President to discuss connectivity and trade
articles.summary: External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar met Myanmar’s President U Min Aung Hlaing to discuss bilateral partnerships in connectivity and trade. The meeting took place during the President's visit to the Mahabodhi Temple in Bihar. The leaders reviewed facets of cooperation as India seeks to maintain regional stability and economic ties.

articles.headline: Migrant labourer from Assam beaten to death in Tamil Nadu
articles.summary: A 35-year-old labourer from Assam, Prosenjit Das, was beaten to death by a mob in Thiruvallur district. Das allegedly misbehaved with a local woman while under the influence of alcohol. Police have launched an investigation into the lynching. Das had recently migrated to the state in search of work.

articles.headline: Social media giants to pay $27 million to settle school lawsuit
articles.summary: Meta, Snap, TikTok, and Google have agreed to a $27 million settlement in a lawsuit filed by a Kentucky school district. The district alleged the platforms are addictive and caused a teen mental health crisis that drained school resources. Meta will pay the largest portion at $9 million, while Snap and TikTok will each pay $8 million.

articles.headline: Oman warns of suspected floating mine in Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Oman’s Maritime Security Centre cautioned seafarers after a floating object suspected to be a naval mine was spotted in the Strait of Hormuz. Authorities urged all vessels and fishermen to exercise extreme caution. The discovery comes amid ongoing regional tensions and previous maritime disruptions in the vital trade waterway.

articles.headline: Americans injured as Iranian ballistic missile strike hits Kuwait air base
articles.summary: An Iranian ballistic missile strike on Kuwait’s Ali Al Salem air base caused minor injuries to five Americans and destroyed two MQ-9 Reaper drones. The drones, valued at $30 million each, were hit by falling debris after Kuwaiti air defenses intercepted the missile. The strike underscores the ongoing risks for US personnel in the region.

articles.headline: Truck carrying Afghan refugees crashes in Afghanistan; 22 killed
articles.summary: A truck carrying Afghan refugees returning from Pakistan overturned on an eastern Afghan highway, killing 22 people and injuring 36 others. The accident highlights the dangers faced by returning refugees in the region, where poor road conditions and vehicle overcrowding frequently lead to fatal traffic incidents.

articles.headline: Aryna Sabalenka and Naomi Osaka set for fourth-round clash
articles.summary: Top seed Aryna Sabalenka reached the French Open fourth round with a win over Daria Kasatkina, setting up a match against former world No. 1 Naomi Osaka. Osaka advanced after a three-set victory over Iva Jovic. Meanwhile, defending champion Coco Gauff crashed out in the third round after a loss to Anastasia Potapova.

articles.headline: Suryakumar and Gill omitted from BCCI’s Asian Games longlist
articles.summary: Suryakumar Yadav and Shubman Gill were missing from the BCCI’s 30-man longlist for the upcoming Asian Games in Aichi-Nagoya. The squad overlap with a home series against West Indies led to the selection of separate teams. Potential captaincy candidates for the Games include Shreyas Iyer, Sanju Samson, and Tilak Varma.

articles.headline: India lose 1-0 to Zimbabwe in Unity Cup third-place play-off
articles.summary: The Indian football team’s Unity Cup campaign ended with a narrow 1-0 defeat to Zimbabwe in London. A 33rd-minute penalty from Prince Dube was enough to secure victory for the African side. The loss relegated India to fourth place in the tournament.

articles.headline: Liverpool sack manager Arne Slot following disastrous title defense
articles.summary: Liverpool have sacked manager Arne Slot, citing a need for a "change of direction" after a poor Premier League season. Bournemouth boss Andoni Iraola is reportedly the frontrunner to take over at Anfield. The decision follows Slot's failure to lead the club to a successful title defense or Champions League qualification.

articles.headline: RIL bets big on green energy; to build gigafactories in Gujarat
articles.summary: Reliance Industries is investing Rs 75,000 crore in Gujarat to build three gigafactories for solar modules, battery storage, and hydrogen. This move signals a significant push into renewable energy and sustainable technologies.

articles.headline: India's Q1 GDP grows 13.5% on robust manufacturing, services
articles.summary: India's Gross Domestic Product (GDP) saw a substantial rise of 13.5% in the first quarter of FY23, driven by strong performance in manufacturing and services sectors, signaling economic recovery.

articles.headline: PM Modi to inaugurate greenfield highway projects worth Rs 29,000 crore
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi is set to inaugurate multiple highway projects in Rajasthan, collectively valued at Rs 29,000 crore, aimed at boosting infrastructure and connectivity.

articles.headline: ADB forecasts India's growth at 7.2% for FY23
articles.summary: The Asian Development Bank has projected India's GDP to grow at 7.2% for the fiscal year 2023, citing resilience in domestic demand and supportive government policies.

articles.headline: New drone rules to boost efficiency, reduce compliance burden
articles.summary: The government has introduced revised drone regulations to simplify approval processes and reduce compliance burdens for drone operators, aiming to foster growth in the sector.

articles.headline: Centre releases Rs 7,200 crore to states for capital expenditure
articles.summary: The Union government has disbursed Rs 7,200 crore to states as interest-free loans to boost capital expenditure, supporting infrastructure development and economic activity.

articles.headline: WHO launches global initiative to accelerate equitable access to vaccines
articles.summary: The World Health Organization has launched a new global initiative to ensure equitable access to vaccines, diagnostics, and therapeutics, particularly for low-income countries.

articles.headline: Adani Group to invest $100 billion in green energy transition
articles.summary: Adani Group plans to invest $100 billion over the next decade in the green energy sector, focusing on renewable power generation and related infrastructure.

articles.headline: New agricultural reforms to boost farmer income
